nativeRange.habitat: Found in marine subtidal zones, typically attached to hard substrate and reef rock in moderate-to-high flow environments.

imageCandidates.notes: Primary product image from Mosaic Macros Pink Plume Weed listing.
Product image from Mosaic Macros Pink Feather Weed listing (same genus).
Product image from Mosaic Macros Chenille Weed listing (same genus).

summary: Pink Plume Weed (Dasya sp.) is a feathery ornamental red alga with intricately branching, plume-like fronds in shades of pink to rose-red. Also sold as Pink Feather Weed and Chenille Weed, it is a display piece best suited to reef tanks with strong, turbulent flow and stable marine water parameters.
careNotes:
Pink Plume Weed belongs to the genus Dasya (family Dasyaceae, order Ceramiales), the same family as the closely related Flamingo Feather (Heterosiphonia gibbesii). The trade names Pink Plume Weed, Pink Feather Weed, and Chenille Weed are all applied to Dasya sp. and share the same fuzzy, intricately branching plume-like growth form. Coloration may vary from pink to rose-red; the Chenille Weed form has been noted in red, pink, or yellow. Strong, turbulent water flow is generally recommended, and water parameters should follow standard reef conditions (temperature 24–27°C, specific gravity 1.023–1.026, pH 7.8–8.4). This species tends to be sensitive to shipping stress and benefits from expedited delivery to ensure live arrival.

Herbivorous fish — including tangs and rabbitfish — along with turbo snails, emerald crabs, and sea urchins tend to graze on delicate red macroalgae and are best kept away from display specimens.

IMPORTANT — DO NOT BURY IN SUBSTRATE: Like other red algae, Pink Plume Weed will MELT at the base if buried in sand. It must be attached to rock using superglue gel, zip-tie or fishing line, or by wedging into a crevice. Keeping the holdfast (the attachment point at the base) above the substrate and in open water flow is essential for long-term health.
breedingNotes: Propagated in the aquarium hobby by fragmentation — separating branch sections and attaching to new substrate. No documented gametophytic reproduction in captive settings.

primary.name: Mosaic Macros
primary.notes: Sole hobby trade source; confirms genus Dasya, plume-like morphology, flow preference, and shipping sensitivity. No numeric water parameters provided.

additional.name: Mosaic Macros — Pink Feather Weed
additional.notes: Consolidated SKU — same genus, feather variant. Confirms Dasya sp., turbulent flow preference, and shipping sensitivity.

additional.name: Mosaic Macros — Chenille Weed
additional.notes: Consolidated SKU — same genus, chenille variant. Notes red/pink/yellow color variation within the genus.

additional.name: AquaMate sibling entry: Flamingo Feather (sw-algae-039)

additional.name: Mosaic Macros general macroalgae care guide
additional.notes: Slice-wide secondary source — provides authoritative Mosaic-wide parameter targets (temp 22-26°C, pH 8.1-8.4, Ca 400-450 ppm, Mg 1250-1350 ppm, NO3 5-20 ppm, PO4 0.02-0.1 ppm) and the 4-method propagation standard. Added retroactively 2026-06-08.
